MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY; REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATION OF MOLLY A. GILARDI; DECLARATION OF SALAYHA GHOURY (TRANSACTION ID # 63341709) FILED BY DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC HEARING SET FOR JUL-08-2019 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY; REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS. NO OPPOSITION FILED. MOTION IS GRANTED AND SANCTIONS IS ORDERED AGAINST PLAINTIFF ROBERT KOPF AND PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY STEVEN JACOBSON, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,125. PLAINTIFF ROBERT KOPF SHALL PROVIDE COMPLETE, VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT UBER'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND REMIT PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANT UBER WITHIN FIVE (5) COURT DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT WLYNN@RROYSELAW.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2018 Continued To Sep-05-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Stay The Case Based On Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Steven R. Jacobsen of the Accidental Lawyer, Tel. No. 1-510-465-1500 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Allison M. Lawrence of Tyson & Mendes, Tel. No. 1-628-253-5074 appearing for the
Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to dismiss or stay based on forum non conveniens is denied. A corporate defendant's principal place of business is presumptively a convenient forum. (Nat'l Football League v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2013) 216 Cal. App. 4th 902, 917.) Where the plaintiff is a California resident, the plaintiff's choice of a forum should rarely be disturbed unless the balance of public and private interest is strongly in favor of the defendant. (Id.) Uber, a California corporate resident, has failed to show that equity strongly favors requiring plaintiff Robert Kopf, a California resident, to litigate his claims in New York. While the incident occurred in New York and potential police witnesses presumably reside in New York, Uber failed to present evidence that the Uber driver and the bystanders who witnessed the incident at an international airport are also residents of New York. The cases relied on by Uber where courts granted a forum non conveniens motion are distinguishable because those cases involved a plaintiff who did not reside in California, but rather resided in the state where the incident occurred. Because Mr. Kopf is a California resident, California has a strong interest in having this case adjudicated in this state. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
